notes: After a clean exit of session S, agy is relaunched with --continue (or --conversation <conv-id>) in the same cwd. LIVE-VERIFIED: agy --continue appends to the ORIGINAL <conv-id> transcript (4→6 lines, no new conv minted), so the same daemon session_id reappears (not a new row); state is ready initially; the first new turn drives ready→working→ready. The arc is asserted per PID lifetime; v2 phases pin same_session_as:v1_session_birth so the resumed turn must fire on the SAME session row. Observations omit cost/tokens (no usage in antigravity's JSONL).

text: First launch: session appears in ready (presession proc-<PID> or conv-id session)
First user prompt drives ready → working
First turn completes back to ready
Clean exit after the first turn
Spec: 'Same session_id reappears (not a new session row)' — agy --continue appends to the same conv-id transcript, so the same session row comes back in ready
Spec: 'ready → working on the first new turn'
Second turn completes back to ready (the cycle is independent across lifetimes)
